title: MongoDB index missing on filtered field
titleAr: فهرس MongoDB مفقود على حقل التصفية
desc: Queries filtering by a non-indexed field force a full collection scan. Add a single-field index for any property used in frequent where clauses.
descAr: استعلامات التصفية بحقل غير مفهرس تجبر على مسح المجموعة كاملة. أضف فهرساً لحقل واحد لكل خاصية تُستخدم في شروط where المتكررة.
summary: A query like .find({ status: 'active' }) on a million documents without an index forces a collection scan that grows linearly with data size. One index turns 500ms reads into 2ms reads.
problem: A query like .find({ status: 'active' }) on a 1M-document collection without an index on status triggers a COLLSCAN (collection scan). Response times degrade from ~2ms with index to >500ms without as the collection grows. Worse, background jobs that poll by status cause CPU spikes every few seconds. The issue is invisible during development with 100 documents, but surfaces in production as the data grows.
problemTitle: Missing index causes full collection scan
solution: Run db.collection.createIndex({ status: 1 }) in the shell or add schema.index({ status: 1 }) in Mongoose. For compound filters create a compound index matching the query shape. Always use explain() to verify IXSCAN vs COLLSCAN. Rule of thumb: any field used in a where clause in frequent queries should have an index.
solutionTitle: Add a single-field index on filtered columns

callout.text: Key idea: use explain('executionStats') before and after adding an index. The totalDocsExamined field should drop from 1M to ~10.

baDesc: The difference in execution stats is dramatic — from scanning every document to reading only matching ones.

before.desc: COLLSCAN reads all 1M documents every query.
